# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

# ======================== Elasticsearch Configuration =========================

# ---------------------------------- Cluster -----------------------------------
cluster.name: production-search-cluster

# ------------------------------------ Node ------------------------------------
node.name: ${HOSTNAME} # Recommended to set unique names like 'node-1', 'node-2', etc.

# ----------------------------------- Network ----------------------------------
# Bind to all network interfaces.
network.host: 0.0.0.0
http.port: 9200
transport.port: 9300

# ---------------------------------- Discovery ---------------------------------
# List of master-eligible nodes to form the cluster. Replace with actual IPs/hostnames.
discovery.seed_hosts: ["192.168.1.10", "192.168.1.11", "192.168.1.12"]

# For the initial bootstrap of a new cluster, list master-eligible nodes.
# This setting is only required on the first startup of a new cluster.
cluster.initial_master_nodes: ["192.168.1.10", "192.168.1.11"]

# ------------------------------------ CORS ------------------------------------
# Enable CORS for cross-origin requests.
# For production, replace "*" with a comma-separated list of allowed origins
# (e.g., "http://your-app.com, https://another-app.com").
http.cors.enabled: true
http.cors.allow-origin: "*"
http.cors.allow-headers: X-Requested-With,Content-Type,Content-Length,Authorization
http.cors.allow-credentials: true

# ------------------------------ Path Settings ---------------------------------
# Uncomment and configure these for production.
# path.data: /var/lib/elasticsearch
# path.logs: /var/log/elasticsearch
